mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate indicates severe economic distress, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate profits, which directly translates to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where UNRATE is above 6.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops to near zero, it signals aggressive monetary easing intended to stimulate the economy, which eventually boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the correlation between FEDFUNDS and SPX returns is not significantly positive in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a broad-based contraction in consumer demand and investment, signaling a deepening recession that will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST both declining in late 2008
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the combined signal fails to outperform a simple average of individual signals.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of significant industrial production contraction, corporate earnings are severely impacted by reduced output and capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as market participants react to new data.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of periods where INDPRO is contracting year-over-year, or the Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant rebound in non-farm payrolls after a period of decline indicates a nascent recovery in the labor market, which typically precedes a broader economic recovery and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 1-month change > 100,000 after 3 consecutive months of decline
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's predictive power is not statistically significant (p-value > 0.1) in recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP, even if slow, provides a fundamental underpinning for corporate profitability and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a longer 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a key economic indicator
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth, or the correlation between GDPC1 growth and SPX returns is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation and increasing federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary environment, which typically leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant in predicting returns.

mechanism_narrative: In a severe recessionary environment, even minor declines in retail sales indicate persistent weakness in consumer demand, which exacerbates negative sentiment and leads to further negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances during a declared recession, or the signal's alpha is not significant during such periods.
